mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and economic growth, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for 3 consecutive months but the S&P 500 still posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of such cases over a 2-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A decisive drop in the unemployment rate below a high threshold signals improving labor market conditions, restoring consumer confidence and spending, which lifts equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 9.0% after having been above 9.5% for at least 3 months
failure_condition: If after each trigger event the S&P 500 60-day forward return is negative in more than 45% of instances, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in housing starts and retail sales indicate synchronized demand growth across consumer durables and housing, signaling a robust expansion that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS month-over-month changes are positive
failure_condition: If in at least 40% of instances where both proxies rise month-over-month the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound (<=0.25%), rising headline CPI inflation is interpreted as a reflation signal that reduces real debt burdens and increases equity valuations, producing positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, if the rank correlation between CPIAUCSL change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is below 0.03 over any 2-year window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate by more than 25 basis points within a quarter signals a deliberate tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses corporate investment, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 25+ bp from the quarterly average to the next quarterly average
failure_condition: If after each trigger event the S&P 500 60-day return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand and rising aggregate income, which immediately boosts consumption and corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the rank correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and forward 5-day SPX return is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate stays below the year-over-year change in CPI, real interest rates are negative, incentivizing investment in equities over fixed income and supporting the S&P 500 over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is less than the 12-month percentage change in CPIAUCSL
failure_condition: If in all periods with negative real rates the average S&P 500 60-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10), reject.

mechanism_narrative: In a housing market recovery regime where housing starts have risen above 600 thousand units from a trough, further increases in housing starts amplify residential investment and create positive wealth effects that lift equity prices over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: During the regime when HOUST averages above 600, if the correlation between HOUST changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (|t| < 1.96) over any 3-year period, reject.
